name: Col de Peyresourde and Port de Balès Loop
description: This difficult 82.3 miles (132.4 km) road cycling loop in the Parc National des Pyrénées features over 10,000 feet of climbing.
region_intro: Hiking around Vignec offers access to the mountainous landscape of the Hautes-Pyrénées, situated at 820 meters above sea level. The region provides diverse terrain, from verdant valleys to significant elevation gains, with panoramic views of the Aure Valley. It serves as a starting point for excursions into the Pyrenees, including segments of the GR10 long-distance hiking trail. Proximity to the Néouvielle National Reserve further enhances the natural features available for exploration. Best hiking trails around Vignec The most popular hiking route is Caneilles Loop Trail, a 2.9 miles (4.7 km) trail that takes 1 hour 24 minutes to complete. This easy route offers charming village views. Another top favourite among local hikers is Vignec – Les Granges de Lias loop from Saint-Lary-Soulan, a moderate 8.8 miles (14.2 km) path. This hike features significant elevation gain and varied terrain. Local hikers also love the Grascoueou Barns – Soulan loop from Vignec, a 4.7 miles (7.5 km) trail leading through mountainous landscapes with panoramic views of the Rioumajou Valley, often completed in about 2 hours 40 minutes. Hiking around Vignec is defined by its Pyrenean mountain ranges, verdant valleys, and access to high-altitude lakes. The network offers options for different ability levels, from easy strolls to more challenging mountain excursions. The routes in Vignec are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.4 stars from over 400 reviews. More than 5000 hikers have used komoot to explore Vignec's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many hiking trails are available around Vignec?
A: Vignec offers a diverse range of hiking opportunities with 18 distinct trails. These include 2 easy routes perfect for a gentle stroll, 13 moderate paths for those seeking a bit more challenge, and 3 difficult trails for experienced hikers looking for significant elevation gains and rugged terrain.

Q: Are there easy hiking trails suitable for beginners or casual walkers in Vignec?
A: Yes, Vignec has several easy trails. A great option for beginners or those looking for a relaxed walk is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e873030770/caneilles-loop-trail" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Caneilles Loop Trail&lt;/a&gt;. This 2.9-mile (4.7 km) route offers charming village views and can be completed in about 1 hour 24 minutes.

Q: What kind of challenging hikes can I find near Vignec?
A: For experienced hikers seeking a challenge, the region around Vignec provides trails with significant elevation gains and mountainous terrain. While specific difficult routes are not detailed here, the Pyrenean landscape ensures demanding ascents and rewarding panoramic views for those ready for a strenuous adventure.

Q: Are there any circular hiking routes in Vignec?
A: Yes, Vignec is well-suited for circular walks. Popular options include the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/20527276" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Vignec – Les Granges de Lias loop from Saint-Lary-Soulan&lt;/a&gt;, which is a moderate 8.8-mile (14.2 km) path with varied terrain. Another excellent choice is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/10528035" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Grascoueou Barns – Soulan loop from Vignec&lt;/a&gt;, a 4.7-mile (7.5 km) trail offering panoramic views of the Rioumajou Valley.

Q: Are the hiking trails around Vignec suitable for families with children?
A: Many trails around Vignec are suitable for families, especially the easier routes. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e873030770/caneilles-loop-trail" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Caneilles Loop Trail&lt;/a&gt; is an excellent family-friendly option, providing pleasant village scenery without being overly strenuous. Always check the difficulty and length of a route to ensure it matches your family's capabilities.

Q: What natural attractions or landmarks can I see along the hiking trails near Vignec?
A: The region around Vignec is rich in natural beauty, particularly its stunning lakes within the Néouvielle National Reserve. Hikers can explore highlights such as &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/1627219" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Lac d'Aubert&lt;/a&gt;, &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/1637275" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Middle Bastan Lake&lt;/a&gt;, and the impressive &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/2472481" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Lake Cap-de-Long&lt;/a&gt;, one of the largest reservoirs in the French Pyrenees. These offer breathtaking views and serene resting spots.

Q: Is Vignec a good destination for wildlife spotting during a hike?
A: Vignec's location within the Hautes-Pyrénées and its proximity to the Néouvielle National Reserve make it an excellent area for observing local wildlife. The diverse mountainous landscape, forests, and high-altitude lakes provide habitats for various species. Keep an eye out for Pyrenean chamois, marmots, and a variety of birdlife, especially in quieter, less frequented areas.

Q: What do other hikers enjoy the most about hiking in Vignec?
A: The hiking experience in Vignec is highly regarded by the komoot community, with an average rating of 4.4 stars from over 400 reviews. Hikers frequently praise the region's stunning Pyrenean mountain ranges, verdant valleys, and the accessibility to high-altitude lakes. The diverse network of trails, catering to all ability levels, is also a major highlight.

Q: What is the best time of year to go hiking in Vignec?
A: The best time to hike in Vignec is generally from late spring through early autumn (May to October) when the weather is mild, and trails are clear of snow. During these months, you'll experience lush landscapes and comfortable temperatures. Some higher-altitude trails may still have snow patches in early spring or late autumn, so always check local conditions.

Q: Are there options for winter hiking or snowshoeing in Vignec?
A: While Vignec is known for its summer hiking, its proximity to the Pyrenees means winter offers opportunities for snowshoeing and winter walks, especially at lower altitudes or on designated trails. The nearby Saint-Lary-Soulan resort provides access to higher areas, which can be starting points for winter activities. Always check snow conditions and avalanche warnings before venturing out in winter.

Q: Where can I find parking for hiking trails in Vignec?
A: Vignec, being a village, typically offers parking options within or at the edge of the village, often near trailheads or public spaces. For specific routes like those starting from Saint-Lary-Soulan or other nearby villages, dedicated parking areas are usually available. It's advisable to check the starting point of your chosen trail for the most convenient parking information.

Q: Can I reach hiking trails in Vignec using public transport?
A: Vignec is a small village, but its adjacency to Saint-Lary-Soulan means there may be public transport connections to the wider valley. While direct public transport to every trailhead might be limited, local bus services often connect villages in the Hautes-Pyrénées. Checking regional transport schedules for connections to Saint-Lary-Soulan or Vignec itself would be the best approach.
tips: A 32-hectare lake in the heart of the Louron valley, in summer it is almost transparent!

The Col de Peyresourde is one of the legendary passes of the Pyrenees. Located at an altitude of 1,589 meters, it marks the separation between the Hautes-Pyrénées and Haute-Garonne departments. The most emblematic climb is the one that starts from Bagnères-de-Luchon. It extends over 13.74 kilometers with an average of 6.9%. It begins with a long false flat before really rising over the last few kilometers.
The port of Balès rises to an altitude of 1,755 meters. The climb from Mauléon-Barousse is 18.9 kilometers long with an average gradient of 6.3%, including some sections at over 14%.
Very beautiful ordeal located in the heights of Nestier. Small hill to get there but it's worth the detour. Nice view over the surrounding countryside.

faqs.question: What is the overall difficulty of the Grand loop of Saint-Lary-Soulan?
faqs.answer: This route is categorized as difficult and requires very good fitness due to its substantial elevation gains and challenging gradients. It's designed for experienced road cyclists.

faqs.question: What kind of terrain can I expect on this cycling route?
faqs.answer: The route features mountainous terrain with significant and often steep climbs. You'll primarily be on well-paved surfaces, though some sections through villages can be narrower, and less-trafficked roads might have bumpier tarmac. Expect challenging gradients, with some sections reaching 9-11%.

faqs.question: What are the main points of interest along the Grand loop of Saint-Lary-Soulan?
faqs.answer: The loop offers spectacular views and passes several notable locations. You'll encounter the View of Lake Génos, iconic mountain passes like Peyresourde Pass and Port de Balès, and the Calvaire du Mont-Arès. The scenery includes deep valleys, forests, pasturelands, and stunning mountain panoramas.

faqs.question: Does this route intersect with any other official cycling or hiking trails?
faqs.answer: Yes, the route shares sections with several other official paths. Notably, it covers about 15% of the Voie d'Aure, 7% of Pedals d'Occitània, and 7% of the Véloroute du Piémont Pyrénéen 03 (Tarbes - Saint-Bertand-de-Comminges). Smaller portions also overlap with La traversée des Pyrénées - sec.5 and the GR 105.

faqs.question: What is the best time of year to cycle the Grand loop of Saint-Lary-Soulan?
faqs.answer: The best time to cycle this route is typically from late spring through early autumn (May to October). During these months, the mountain passes are generally clear of snow, and the weather is more favorable for cycling. Always check local weather conditions before you go, especially for high-altitude sections.

faqs.question: Are dogs allowed on the Grand loop of Saint-Lary-Soulan?
faqs.answer: As this is a road cycling route primarily on public roads, there are no specific restrictions against dogs. However, due to the challenging nature of the ride and the presence of traffic, it is not recommended to bring dogs along on the bike. If you plan to stop at any natural sites or villages, local regulations for dogs should be observed.

faqs.question: Do I need any special permits or pay entrance fees for this route?
faqs.answer: No, there are no specific permits or entrance fees required to cycle the Grand loop of Saint-Lary-Soulan itself, as it primarily uses public roads. However, parts of the route may pass through or near the Parc National des Pyrénées. While cycling on main roads within the park generally doesn't require special permits, always respect park regulations regarding wildlife, waste, and general conduct.

faqs.question: Where can I park to start this cycling loop?
faqs.answer: Saint-Lary-Soulan serves as an excellent starting and ending point for this loop. You can find various public parking options within the town. It's advisable to check for specific parking areas suitable for longer stays, especially during peak season.

faqs.question: Is the Grand loop of Saint-Lary-Soulan suitable for beginners?
faqs.answer: No, this route is not suitable for beginners. With over 132 km and more than 3,100 meters of elevation gain, it is rated as 'difficult' and requires very good fitness and experience with challenging mountain climbs. Beginners should look for shorter, flatter routes in the valley.

faqs.question: What should I bring for a day of cycling on this route?
faqs.answer: Given the route's length and difficulty, you should bring appropriate road cycling gear, including a well-maintained racebike, helmet, cycling apparel, and plenty of water and nutrition. Be prepared for changing mountain weather with layers, and carry essential repair tools, a first-aid kit, and a fully charged phone. Sun protection is also crucial.

faqs.question: Are there places to refill water or find food along the way?
faqs.answer: The route passes through several villages and mountain passes where you may find opportunities to refill water bottles or purchase food. Notable passes like Peyresourde Pass often have facilities. However, it's always wise to carry sufficient supplies, especially on longer, more remote sections, as services may not be consistently available or open.
